Commercial Apr 2, 2026

Here’s How Much Wider The Airbus A350 Cabin Is Compared To The Boeing 787 Dreamliner

The Airbus A350 cabin is 5 inches wider than the Boeing 787—here’s how that small difference transforms seat comfort and long-haul travel.

The A350's 5-inch cabin width advantage enables three-abreast business class configurations versus two-abreast on the 787, directly impacting airline revenue per seat on premium routes.

See Simple Flying →

Regulatory Apr 2, 2026

Washington Repeals Aircraft Tax Before It Takes Effect

Measure enacted in 2025 is replaced with fuel tax and fee increases.

Washington's shift from aircraft-specific taxation to fuel and fee structures may create more competitive pricing for in-state operators while redistributing cost burden across broader aviation activities.

See AVweb →

Military Apr 2, 2026

Global Combat Air Programme Awards First Contract with Edgewing

The first joint international contract has been awarded for the Global Combat Air Programme (GCAP), focusing on key design and engineering activities. The GCAP International Government Organisation (GIGO), which represents the governments of the UK, Italy and Japan, awarded a £686 million contract...

The £686 million initial GCAP contract signals that tripartite defense cooperation between UK, Italy, and Japan is moving from planning to execution on a next-generation fighter program.

See The Aviationist →

Safety Apr 2, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern aviation SMS shifts the industry paradigm from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically identifying and mitigating hazards before they cause incidents.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

International Apr 2, 2026

How the Iran War Is Rewiring Avionics

Global Avionics Round-Up from Aircraft Value News (AVN) The latest Middle East conflict has done what decades of airline risk modeling feared but never fully priced: it has snapped the […]...

The Iran conflict is forcing airlines to reassess geopolitical risk premiums in avionics procurement and routing costs that traditional models underestimated.

See Aviation Today →

Industry Apr 2, 2026

Silicon Over Steel: The Avionics Reset That Stole the Show at ISTAT Americas 2026

Global Avionics Round-Up from Aircraft Value News (AVN) From my perspective in covering the show as a reporter, the biggest avionics story to emerge from ISTAT Americas 2026 wasn’t a […]...

Semiconductor-based avionics are replacing traditional metal-core systems, signaling a fundamental architecture shift that will reshape maintenance, certification, and lifecycle economics across commercial fleets.

See Aviation Today →

Safety Apr 2, 2026

Climate Change Is Rewriting the Rules of Turbulence—and Cockpits Are Playing Catch-Up

Global Avionics Round-Up from Aircraft Value News (AVN) For decades, turbulence was treated as a known nuisance—unpredictable, occasionally dangerous, but broadly manageable within the design assumptions of modern aircraft. Engineers […]...

Climate-driven turbulence changes are exceeding historical design parameters, forcing avionics and aircraft systems to evolve beyond decades-old assumptions.

See Aviation Today →

Safety Apr 2, 2026

Safety Culture in Aviation SMS

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

SMS effectiveness depends less on process documentation than on psychological safety enabling frontline employees to report hazards without fear of retaliation.

Military Apr 2, 2026

Army’s SkyFoundry Plan To Build Own Cheap Drones Has ‘Evolved,’ Official Notes Commercial Involvement

The Army’s SkyFoundry initiative to manufacture thousands of its own cheap drones on a monthly basis has recently “evolved,” with an official telling sister publication Defense Daily the program is “100 […]...

The Army's shift toward commercial partnerships in SkyFoundry signals a strategic pivot from vertical integration toward leveraging private sector manufacturing capabilities and cost efficiencies.

See Aviation Today →

Military Apr 2, 2026

With an Eye To CCA Increment 2, Talon IQ Conducts Test Flight

A “Talon IQ” Model 437 aircraft by Northrop Grumman‘s Scaled Composites has flown a mission autonomy test flight using the company’s Prism software and Hivemind software by Shield AI, the companies […]...

Northrop Grumman's autonomous flight integration with Shield AI's Hivemind software represents a critical step toward meeting military requirements for unmanned aircraft autonomy in contested environments.

See Aviation Today →

Military Apr 2, 2026

Army Accepts First Optionally-Piloted Black Hawk, Will Inform Scaling Autonomy Across The Fleet

The Army announced Friday it has officially accepted the first Black Hawk helicopter outfitted to fly autonomously, with the plans for the aircraft to now undergo a “rigorous testing phase.” […]...

The Army's autonomous Black Hawk acceptance establishes a critical validation pathway that could accelerate unmanned helicopter adoption across military rotorcraft operations.

See Aviation Today →

General Aviation Apr 2, 2026

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

Eastern Iowa Airport's mixed-use model demonstrates how regional hubs can sustain both flight training operations and commercial service, generating year-round revenue across multiple aviation sectors.

The 49.9% Question: Inside Air France-KLM’s Strategic Play for TAP Air Portugal

Air France-KLM has moved first in the TAP battle, and that could prove decisive.

Air France-KLM's pre-emptive stake in TAP positions it to shape European consolidation before competitors can act, potentially reshaping Iberian aviation networks.

Qatar Airways Suspends Flights To 64 Destinations [Full List & Map]

All airlines in the Gulf have been affected, and Qatar Airways is no exception. See all the destinations that are currently suspended.

Regional geopolitical tensions are creating operational ripple effects across Gulf carriers, forcing Qatar Airways to rationalize its network during a critical recovery period.

Aerospace Apr 2, 2026

Moog Technology Successfully Steers Artemis II Launch

East Aurora, NY — Moog Inc. (NYSE: MOG.A and MOG.B), a worldwide designer, manufacturer, and systems integrator of high-performance precision motion and fluid controls and control systems, provided the critical […]...

Moog's precision motion control systems are essential to NASA's deep space exploration infrastructure, positioning suppliers of advanced guidance technology as irreplaceable to human spaceflight missions.

Safety Apr 2, 2026

RNO Mustang 9 Departure

Pilots departing from runways 17 left and right at Reno-Tahoe International need to pay special attention to the Mustang 9 Departure. It is extremely important that the procedure on the Mustang 9...

The Mustang 9 Departure from RNO's parallel runways 17L/R requires heightened procedural compliance to avoid terrain and airspace hazards common to high-altitude Nevada operations.

Commercial Apr 2, 2026

Gone After Just 1 Year: Virgin Atlantic Axes This Long-Haul Route From London

Virgin Atlantic’s Riyadh exit looks abrupt, but the bigger story is where the aircraft goes next.

Virgin Atlantic's rapid withdrawal from Riyadh signals capacity reallocation challenges as carriers reassess long-haul profitability in competitive Gulf markets amid shifting demand patterns.

Discover the highest-paid US Air Force pilots in 2026, including ranks, bonuses, and total salaries for top-earning military aviators.

Air Force pilot compensation structures now include performance-based bonuses and retention incentives, reflecting increased competition for experienced aviators amid pilot shortage pressures.

Carmel Ortiz on the evolving landscape of satellite communications

In this week’s episode of Space Minds, host Mike Gruss interviews Carmel Ortiz, senior vice president of medium-Earth-orbit constellation programs at SES. They discuss new technology to help satellites communicate […]...

MEO satellite constellations are emerging as a competitive alternative to LEO networks, offering lower latency than GEO while reducing the scale required compared to LEO deployments.

Industry Apr 2, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across the entire aviation system can simultaneously address capacity constraints and environmental concerns without requiring complete fleet replacement.

See MDPI →

Electric Apr 2, 2026

FAA Clears Key Step for ZeroAvia Electric Engine

Publication of special conditions marks important certification step for electric regional propulsion system.

ZeroAvia's special conditions approval signals FAA readiness to establish certification pathways for electric regional aircraft, potentially accelerating decarbonization of 19-seat to 75-seat market segments.

See AVweb →

Commercial Apr 2, 2026

5 Reasons Why The Boeing 777X Needs Folding Wingtips

The Boeing 777X's folding wingtips are a critical solution to a fundamental design conflict between airline efficiency and airport infrastructure.

Folding wingtips solve a critical constraint: airlines demand larger, more efficient aircraft but most airports cannot accommodate the 777X's 235-foot wingspan without costly infrastructure upgrades.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 2, 2026

Lufthansa submits non-binding offer for minority stake in TAP Air Portugal

Lufthansa Group has submitted a non-binding offer to Portugal’s state holding company Parpublica for a minority stake in flag carrier TAP Air…...

Lufthansa's minority stake bid signals consolidation in European aviation as carriers seek strategic partnerships to navigate post-pandemic recovery and operational efficiency.

Regulatory Apr 2, 2026

FAA Absent from Cleveland Burke Lakefront Closure Hearing

City officials cite costs and redevelopment potential, while others raise concerns over process and economic impact.

The FAA's absence from a critical airport closure hearing suggests potential gaps in federal oversight of local airfield decisions that could affect regional aviation infrastructure and economic activity.

Commercial Apr 2, 2026

Why Seat Width & Pitch Vary So Much Between US Airlines

No two airlines in the US are really quite the same, especially in terms of seats. What makes each airline so drastically different?

Airlines optimize seat configurations independently without federal standards, allowing carriers to extract maximum revenue from identical aircraft types through varying density strategies.

Commercial Apr 2, 2026

Steven Greenway steps down as flyadeal CEO after two years

Steven Greenway, the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of flyadeal, announced on April 2, 2026, that he is stepping…...

Leadership transitions at regional carriers like flyadeal signal potential strategic shifts in ultra-low-cost carrier positioning within the Middle East market.

Commercial Apr 2, 2026

How Rolls-Royce Engines Differ From General Electric Engines

Both engine manufacturers power some of the world's largest commercial jets.

Rolls-Royce and GE engines differ primarily in bypass ratio design philosophy and maintenance intervals, affecting airline operating costs and fleet commonality strategies.

Aerospace Apr 2, 2026

Phantom Space buys thermal specialist to support orbital data center push

Phantom Space has acquired satellite thermal hardware provider Thermal Management Technologies to bolster development of its planned Phantom Cloud orbital data center constellation.

Vertical integration into thermal management addresses a critical engineering bottleneck for orbital data centers operating in extreme temperature environments.

Press Release: Latitude Interiors Group inks pact with N.A. regional

Latitude Interiors Group, a leader in aircraft interior solutions, today announced the signing of a new long-term agreement with a major regional airline in North America, further solidifying its position as a comprehensive, end-to-end interiors partner for the aviation industry. Under the...

Long-term interiors contracts with regional carriers provide stable revenue streams and demonstrate market consolidation toward integrated cabin solution providers.

Aerospace Apr 2, 2026

Relativity, Hermeus, Astrion and Divergent executives join Fortastra C-suite

SAN FRANCISCO – Los Angeles startup Fortastra has hired veterans from Relativity Space, Hermeus, Astrion and Divergent Technologies to design and operate maneuverable spacecraft for on-orbit security. Josh Jetter, former Relativity senior director of avionics engineering and manufacturing, will be...

Fortastra's recruitment of advanced manufacturing and aerospace leadership signals growing private sector focus on autonomous orbital security capabilities beyond traditional satellite operations.

The lucky travelers flying from Costa Rica to Atlanta were in the right place at the right time.

Unplanned orbital viewing opportunities demonstrate how cruise altitude positioning can create unique passenger experiences that enhance brand loyalty without additional airline investment.

Military Apr 2, 2026

CENTCOM Confirms EA-37B Compass Call Deployed as Part of Operation Epic Fury

The EA-37B was added to the list of assets involved in Operation Epic Fury, although the aircraft have not yet arrived in the CENTCOM AOR. Following the arrival of two EA-37B Compass Call electronic warfare aircraft at RAF Mildenhall, UK, on Mar. 31, 2026, the U.S. Central Command has now confirmed...

The EA-37B Compass Call's deployment signals escalating electronic warfare capabilities in CENTCOM operations, with aircraft positioning in the UK indicating broader regional conflict preparation.

Military Apr 2, 2026

New Photos Provide Clearest View Yet of Secretive NT-43A RAT55’s Dorsal Turret and Enclosure

The deployment of the one-of-a-kind 737 to MacDill Air Force Base, Florida, to support the Artemis II launch, provided aircraft spotters and photographers with an unprecedented opportunity for a closer look at the NT-43A “RAT 55” and its dorsal EO/IR turret. As we reported in detail yesterday, the...

The NT-43A's deployment to MacDill AFB reveals advanced electro-optical/infrared reconnaissance capabilities that directly support critical space launch operations, indicating growing integration of specialized airborne intelligence platforms with NASA missions.
